value.found_quote: Workflows enable you to define a series of steps to guide the Agent through a repetitive set of tasks, such as deploying a service or responding to PR comments. These Workflows are saved as markdown files, allowing you to have an easy repeatable way to run key processes. Once saved, Workflows can be invoked in Agent via a slash command with the format /workflow-name.
value.rationale: The page explicitly describes Workflows as saved markdown files that define a repeatable series of steps to guide the Agent through tasks. The claim accurately characterizes the feature: Workflows are markdown files, they define a repeatable series of steps, and they guide the Agent through tasks. The page does not indicate any preview/beta/deprecated status — it is presented as a current, available feature with full usage instructions. The only minor nuance is the claim omits mention of cross-session persistence being explicitly stated, but the page does describe them as saved files invokable via slash commands, implying persistence across sessions.
